designConcept.summary:
## Design Concept — library-unified-view

**Goal:** Recast the Library tab as a single unified view of all content syllago can reach — local library items, Project Content, and Registry Clone items not yet Added — with state-based filters, inline action buttons, and matching CLI parity, so users have one place to discover and act on any content regardless of source.

**Scope:**
- Library tab renders Registry Clone items inline with Library items; not-yet-Added items display in muted styling with a `[not in library]` state indicator
- Filter chips: `in-library`, `not-in-library`, `installed`, `not-installed`, `project`; default unfiltered
- `syllago list --filter=<state>` repeatable flag + `[library]` tag column
- Metapanel buttons per state: `[a] Add` + `[i] Add + Install` for not-in-library; existing `[i]` / `[x]` / `[d]` / `[e]` for Library items; `[e] Edit` hidden when item.Library == false; `[u] Update` reserved for outdated registry-sourced Library items
- `syllago add --install --to <provider>` chains add.AddItems + installer.Install; no new orchestrator
- Post-registry-add TUI modal + CLI hint; both suppress via hints.registry_add_dismissed=true
- Updated INSTALL_002 / ITEM_001 suggestion text for registry-only-not-in-library case
- Mouse + keyboard parity, telemetry PropertyDefs, golden file updates

**Out of scope:**
- Auto-copying registry content to Library on registry add
- Replacing the two-step add→install model
- New error codes; new orchestrator package; Project Content detection changes

**User-visible behavior:**
Library tab shows unified list — Library items normally, Project Content with [project] badge, not-yet-Added Registry Clone items muted with [not in library]. Filter chips narrow by state. Not-in-library items show [a] Add and [i] Add + Install in metapanel; Library items keep existing buttons. registry add triggers a one-time modal/hint explaining the add-before-use requirement, suppressible via config. syllago install <registry-only-item> emits updated error with copy-pasteable syllago add command.

**Key decisions:**
- Unified view vs. tabs: unified + filter chips — tabs force users to know which source to check
- Add+Install: --install --to flag on existing syllago add — no new orchestrator layer needed
- Error UX: suggestion text only on existing codes — avoids breaking callers JSON handling
- Suppression: hints.registry_add_dismissed in ~/.syllago/config.json via syllago config set — no new persistence layer
- [u] Update: runs registry sync first, re-copies only if newer — matches existing sync semantics

**Risks / open questions accepted:**
- Large registries could produce unbounded row counts; pagination/virtual scroll deferred
- [u] Update conflict behavior (local edits to a registry-sourced Library item) deferred

**Depends on / affects:** catalog, tui (Library tab, metapanel, golden files), list_cmd, add_cmd, registry_cmd, installer, telemetry/catalog.go, ~/.syllago/config.json

researchQuestions: How does app.go currently pass items to the library sub-model SetItems? What is the exact call site and what slice does it pass?
What fields on catalog.ContentItem discriminate between Library items, Project Content, and Registry Clone items — and what are their exact types, zero-values, and invariants?
How are the current action buttons rendered in metapanel.go? What is the full conditional block that decides which buttons appear, including isUnstagedRegistryItem?
How does list_cmd.go currently filter and output items — what flags exist, how is per-row output formatted, and where is the output loop?
What flags does add_cmd.go currently accept, and how does the install step (if any) get triggered today — what is the entry point into installer.Install from the add pipeline?
In registry_cmd.go and actions.go, what is the full success path after a registry add — where does control return and what output is emitted?

How are INSTALL_002 and ITEM_001 errors currently constructed and emitted — what is the call site, what does the suggestion field currently contain, and are there any callers that branch on the error code?
How does syllago currently read and write ~/.syllago/config.json — what struct/package owns it, and how are boolean flags persisted and read back?
